subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, engaging title that establishes focus
Comprehensive table of contents and consistent use of headings
Generally coherent progression from rules to probability to expectedâvalue analysis
weaknesses: Overly long rule descriptions dilute focus; could be more concise
Some unnecessary repetition of background material and EV formulae
Placeholder header (âXXXâ) in the softâtotals table needs meaningful label
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands scored: Coherence/logical development band 3, Organization band 3, Relevance/conciseness band 2. The structure is clear and the logical flow is generally maintained, but unnecessary background and occasional jumps (e.g., EV tables before derivation) prevent topâband refinement. A score of 3 balances strong organisation with areas needing pruning.

strengths: Clear statement of aims with accurate probability calculations and percentage conversions
Effective use of tables (bust probabilities, basic strategy, soft totals) to convey results
Succinct legend explaining symbols aids interpretation and clarity
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (mixing boldface, overlines, subscripts) without definition
Lack of general summation notation (e.g., â páµ¢ váµ¢) before numeric EV examples
Missing captions or labels for figures (deck image) weaken integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication strands scored: Language/notation band 2, Representations band 2, Logical structure/clarity band 3. The logical flow is good and tables are well presented, but inconsistent notation and missing general forms limit this criterion to a solid band 3 holistically.

strengths: Topic choice shows some personal interest
Inclusion of selected EV calculations indicates minimal original effort
weaknesses: Approach follows standard published analyses with little personal angle
Very limited independent thinking or novel methodology
No evidence of creative extensions (e.g., simulation or coding)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All Personal Engagement strands (independent thinking, personal approach, creativity/initiative) are at band 1: the work shows only basic engagement with commonplace methods and lacks deeper personal or innovative contributions.

weaknesses: No discussion of limitations (e.g., infiniteâdeck assumption)
Very superficial analysis of results without exploring why probabilities differ
No future implications, extensions, or quantitative evaluation of outcomes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands (depth, critical analysis, evaluation) are at band 1: only a brief, general conclusion is offered without meaningful or critical discussion of assumptions, limitations, or next steps.

strengths: Relevant use of combinatorics and probability to justify strategy choices
Mathematics commensurate with SL level (combinations, probability, EV)
Accurate combinatorial counting (e.g., 32 bust cards) supports probability reasoning
weaknesses: Inconsistent deck assumptions (singleâdeck vs infinite deck) need rigorous justification
Unexplained payoff value (â0.45) and missing intermediate probabilities
EV tables lack derivation steps and summation notation for full transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands scored: Relevance band 3, Level appropriateness band 3, Understanding/accuracy band 2. The mathematics is appropriate and correctly applied in places, but conceptual inconsistencies and omitted steps limit the work to a band 3 overall.

strandSelections.reasoning: Paragraphs usually start with a clear aim, calculations are then shown, and a decision (hit/stand) is stated, giving a recognisable logical chain.  Although derivations are sometimes thin, the overall communication is clear enough to merit the âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ band.

The student shows some understanding of probability and EV, but there are conceptual inaccuracies: treating the deck as infinite while quoting single-deck counts, unexplained â0.45 pay-off, and rounding/formatting slips.  These errors indicate partial, not full, accuracy; âsome understanding with errorsâ (band 2) is therefore appropriate.

The student employs tables for bust probabilities and basic-strategy charts, and includes one image of a deck; these count as different representations, but graphs or tree diagrams that would deepen understanding are absent.  Hence âsome use of different forms of representationâ is appropriate.

The exploration follows the classic report structure (title, contents, numbered sections, conclusion) and ideas progress in a generally logical orderâfrom rules, to probability calculations, to expected-value tables and examples.  Although occasional jumps occur (e.g., EV tables introduced before their derivation is explained) the reader can still follow the argument without major difficulty.  Hence the work is coherent and logically developed but not refined enough for the top band.

Aside from compiling tables, the work shows little creativity or initiative; no simulation, code, or novel model is attempted.  Consequently the minimum band with âsome creativity or initiativeâ is most fitting.

The student comments superficially that EV shows one option is better, but provides little critical analysis of why probabilities differ or how assumptions affect results.  Hence only basic analysis is evident.

Outcome evaluation is limited to stating that mathematics helps maximise winning chances.  No quantitative measure of improvement, nor future research suggestions, are discussed.  Thus evaluation remains limited.

Reflection is confined to a brief concluding paragraph that reiterates findings in general terms.  There is no consideration of limitations (e.g., infinite-deck assumption) or alternative strategies.  This meets only âlimited reflectionâ.

Basic probability notation (P(Blackjack)=â¦) and fraction/percentage conversions are correctly used, but symbols are sometimes informal or inconsistent (mixing boldface, overlines, subscripts with no definition).  EV formulae omit expected-value summations and use ad-hoc numbers such as â0.45 without explanation.  Therefore only âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ is achieved.

The approach is largely generic: the student follows commonly published blackjack analyses without introducing a distinctive methodology or personal twist.  A short personal-interest statement is given, but deeper personal direction is missing.

Most material is relevant, but there is some unnecessary background (full rule description, repeated legend explanations, duplicated EV formulae).  The write-up is occasionally wordy and the assumption of an âinfinite deckâ contradicts the earlier single-deck statement, signalling irrelevancy and lack of pruning.  Thus the strand fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

The mathematics (basic combinatorics, simple probability, expected-value arithmetic) is relevant to the question and used appropriately to justify strategic choices.  However, only a subset of blackjack complexities is addressed, so comprehensiveness is lacking; band 3 (ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ) fits best.

Independent thinking is limited to choosing the topic and selecting a few example EV calculations.  Most probability values and basic-strategy tables are standard and appear copied from external sources with little original adaptation.

A table of contents is provided and headings/sub-headings are used consistently.  Sections are sensibly ordered and page numbers referenced, showing clear organisation.  Minor issues such as the conclusion being embedded inside section 4 instead of a separate numbered section prevent it from being âexceptionallyâ organised.

Probability of events, combinations, and EV lie well within the SL course.  The student applies these at a standard level but does not extend beyond it (e.g., conditional probabilities after card removal).  Hence mathematics is âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3).

annotations.comment: The title is clear, engaging and establishes the focus of the exploration effectively.
The table of contents is comprehensive and helps the reader navigate the IA, showing good use of structure.
The introduction provides general context but includes long rule descriptions that dilute focusâconsider summarizing rules more concisely.
The motivation statement describes popularity but lacks a personal insight or unique angleâembed your own experience or interest.
There is an inconsistency between using a single deck and later treating the deck as infiniteâclarify or justify this assumption rigorously.
The deck image lacks a descriptive caption explaining its relevanceâadd brief labels or references to tie it into your assumptions.
The list of assumptions mixes symbols (â , â¡) without definitionsâensure all notation is introduced and explained.
Presenting the combination count is solid; to deepen communication, include the general form $$\binom{n}{k}$$ before substituting values.
The probability calculation is accurate and clearly shown with percentage conversionâgood mathematical representation.
Typo in the equation label (âbusingâ instead of âbustingâ); correct the text to maintain professional mathematical language.
Enumerating 32 bust cards is precise and supports the probability reasoning wellâdemonstrates accurate combinatorial counting.
The bust-probability table is clear but could be streamlined by removing repetitive % formatting in the body for conciseness.
The commentary suggests a 50% cutoff but doesnât analyze why EV differsâconsider adding critical reflection on this threshold.
The basic strategy table uses mixed font styles and overlines inconsistently in headersâstandardize notation for clarity.
The legend succinctly explains symbols, aiding interpretationâeffective use of mathematical communication.
There is no derivation or justification of the basic strategy EV tableâinclude a brief outline of how probabilities feed into each table cell.
The unreferenced payoff of â0.45 in the EV(Hit) calculation needs a clear originâexplain its source or derivation.
Use a summation notation $$EV=\sum p_i v_i$$ to generalize expected-value calculations before plugging in numbers for clarity.
The header âXXXâ in the softâtotals table is a placeholderâreplace with meaningful row heading (e.g., âHandâ).
Soft totals table is well presented, offering multiple representations of strategyâeffective mathematical communication.
In the softâ18 EV example you conclude to stand âto be safeâ without mathematical justificationâreflect on risk and assumptions.
The EV calculations for soft totals are accurate but omit intermediate probabilitiesâshow full steps to strengthen use of mathematics.
The conclusion restates findings but lacks discussion of limitations or possible extensionsâadd critical reflection on assumptions like the infinite deck.
The conclusion attributes strategy to a âspecial approachâ but does not tie back to your unique engagementâconsider highlighting your personal contribution.
References include a ChatGPT conversationâensure the reliability and consistent formatting of each citation according to a recognized style.
Math symbols appear inline in references (e.g., \omega_{\rm{max}}) without purposeâremove or explain these extraneous notations.
